title: A Song Prayer
content:
Lord Jesus,
        Oh, ease us
        Of Self that oppresses,
        Annoys and distresses
        Body and brain
        With dull pain!
        Thou never,
        Since ever,
        Save one moment only,
        Wast left, or wast lonely:
        We are alone,
        And make moan.

        Far parted,
        Dull-hearted,
        We wander, sleep-walking,
        Mere shadows, dim-stalking:
        Orphans we roam,
        Far from home.

        Oh new man,
        Sole human,
        God's son, and our brother,
        Give each to the other--
        No one left out
        In cold doubt!

        High Father,
        Oh gather
        Thy sons and thy daughters,
        Through fires and through waters,
        Home to the nest
        Of thy breast!

        There under
        The wonder
        Of great wings of healing,
        Of love and revealing,
        Teach us anew
        To sing true.
meaning:
"A Song Prayer" is a quintessential example of George MacDonald's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
